text.title: Ops Automation Workflow Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Ops Automation Workflow. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Inventory existing manual tasks + existing automations before designing a new one. Build without inventory creates duplicates.
rule.rationale: Without inventory the backlog double-counts and conflicts.

rule.statement: Each candidate carries ROI = (hours_saved/month * cost_per_hour) - tool_cost. Rank backlog by ROI descending.
rule.rationale: Unranked automation backlogs default to 'whatever's loudest', not highest payback.

rule.statement: Each running automation has a named human owner — never 'team'.
rule.rationale: Anonymous automations rot silently for months.

rule.statement: Each automation has at least one observable health signal (success counter, error notification, dashboard widget).
rule.rationale: Silent failures of unobserved automations are the dominant failure mode.

rule.statement: Quarterly review: automations below ROI threshold or broken >30 days are retired, not patched indefinitely.
rule.rationale: Sprawl accumulates without a forced cull.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ops-automation-workflow.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
